title: aglaia elliptica
intro: Aglaia elliptica, also known as the Thai bitters or kuapu, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, which is native to Southeast Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use as a bitter tonic, which stimulates digestion and relieve flatulence. The health benefits of Aglaia elliptica include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism, as well as its potential to protect against certain types of cancer. Aglaia elliptica is often used in herbal preparations, such as tea and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or extracting the active compounds with a solvent like ethanol.
